title: phalacroseris bolanderi
intro: Phalacroseris bolanderi, also known as the Bolander's Island Lake-aster, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Phalacroseris bolanderi include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of Phalacroseris bolanderi can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
